name: chart
title: Chart
description: Themed chart shell on Recharts — CSS-variable colours, a config-driven tooltip that always carries units, and an accessible tabular equivalent of the plotted data.

props.name: config
props.description: Per-series `label`, `unit`, `color`, `icon`. `unit` drives the tooltip — a clinical value without its unit is unreadable.

props.name: label
props.description: Required. Names the chart for assistive tech; an unlabelled plot is unreadable to a screen reader.

props.name: dataTable
props.description: The plotted values as a table — use ChartDataTable. Visually hidden unless `showTable`.

props.name: showTable
props.description: Reveals the data table below the chart.

props.name: caption
props.description: Units, the period, or the source of a reference range.

props.name: children
props.description: A single Recharts element (LineChart, AreaChart, BarChart…).

props.name: ChartDataTable: caption / columns / rows / missingLabel
props.description: `missingLabel` renders where a value was not measured — never "0", never blank.

clinicalNotes: The container renders the series it is given: it does not resample, interpolate across gaps, or smooth, because a smoothed line through missing observations is a measurement that was never taken. Two rules are structural rather than optional. Units live in the series config so the tooltip cannot omit them — unit ambiguity (mg/dL vs mmol/L, kg vs lb) is a documented source of clinical harm. And every chart carries a tabular equivalent, because a screen-reader user gets nothing from an SVG path; ChartDataTable renders "Not measured" for absent values so a gap in charting never reads as a zero. Where a value is critical, flag it by shape and label as well as colour — a colour-blind reader, or a printed chart, must convey the same warning.
notes: The foundation of the Clinical Data round: reference-range-band, the trend charts, and the flowsheet all compose it. Hand-drawn SVG components (ecg-strip, trend-sparkline) deliberately do not — a waveform and an inline glyph are the wrong jobs for a charting library.
